title: oxalis subvillosa
intro: Oxalis subvillosa, also known as the hairy wood-sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The hairy wood-sorrel has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its rich content of oxalic acid and other compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as an antiseptic, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, oxalis subvillosa is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
